title: Commissioner of Income Tax v. Calcutta Knitwears
legal_principles: Survey statements are not conclusive admissions
Right to retract statements made during survey
summary: The Supreme Court held that statements recorded during a survey under Section 133A of Income Tax Act cannot be used as conclusive admissions. Assessee has right to retract such statements and department must independently verify income through evidence.
key_holdings: Statement recorded under Section 133A during survey is not a sworn statement
Assessee can retract statement made during survey
Tax authorities cannot solely rely on survey statements — must corroborate with other evidence
Voluntary disclosure has evidentiary value but is not conclusive
why_important: Protects taxpayers from coerced admissions during income tax surveys. Clarifies evidentiary weight of survey statements.
relevance_to_legal_finance_system: Directly relevant to tax assessment, audit compliance, and rights of taxpayers during IT surveys.
